title: ceterach haughtonii
intro: Ceterach haughtonii, also known as the Irish fern, is a type of lycopod that belongs to the family Selaginellaceae, characterized by its small, scale-like leaves. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Ceterach haughtonii include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Ceterach haughtonii is often combined with other plants, such as thyme and eucalyptus, to create teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to alleviate respiratory symptoms.
